keyPoints: ggplot2 is the primary and most powerful package for creating complex, layered graphics based on The Grammar of Graphics.
Lattice is useful for creating multi-panel conditioning plots, especially for visualizing multivariate data.
Plotly can be used to create interactive web-based graphics from ggplot2 or lattice plots.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add layers with the '+' operator in ggplot2, resulting in an empty or incomplete plot.
Using base R graphics for a complex, publication-quality figure when ggplot2 would be more appropriate and efficient.
Confusing the syntax and data structure requirements (e.g., long vs. wide format) between the different graphing paradigms.
